name: Dow
description_short: Dow Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution serving nearly 84,000 members across all 50 states, offering deposit accounts, loans, credit cards, and investment services with competitive rates.
description_long:
Dow Credit Union is a not-for-profit, member-owned credit union based in the Great Lakes Bay Region (Michigan). Founded in 1980, it has grown to become the largest and fastest-growing credit union in its region with membership spanning all 50 states and 40 countries. The institution has received national recognition from the Wall Street Journal and Newsweek, and has returned over $300 million to members through year-end rebates and rewards since its founding.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), high-yield deposit accounts, auto loans, recreational vehicle loans, personal loans, mortgages, and Visa credit cards. They feature competitive deposit rates, with CDs offering APY as high as 4.00% and high-yield deposits at 3.51% APY. Members benefit from a low-rate Visa credit card designed for balance transfers and everyday purchases, along with access to an online Retirement & Investment Center and wealth planning services.

Dow Credit Union distinguishes itself through member-centric policies and community commitment. The institution charges $0 NSF (non-sufficient funds) fees, which is uncommon in the banking industry. They actively give back to members through regular year-end rebates and rewards programs. The credit union emphasizes quick loan approval processes and offers exclusive rate discounts for members who establish direct deposits. Their "Stay Safe" educational series helps members identify and prevent financial scams, demonstrating a commitment to financial literacy.

Based on website content, Dow Credit Union appears to be a legitimate, well-established financial institution with strong member satisfaction reflected in testimonials from long-term members (some with 50+ years of membership). However, prospective members should note that not all products qualify for Member Giveback programs, some loan approvals are subject to conditions, and membership eligibility criteria are not fully detailed on the homepage.
services: Certificates of deposit (CDs) with APY up to 4.00%
Visa credit cards with low rates
pros: CD rates as high as 4.00% APY and high-yield deposits at 3.51% APY
Over $300 million returned to members since 1980 through year-end rebates
Quick loan approval process with rates available for auto, RV, and personal loans
Exclusive rate discounts available for members who set up direct deposit
Serves all 50 states and 40 countries with nearly 84,000 members
Membership open to all (not restricted by employer or geography)
National recognition from Wall Street Journal and Newsweek
cons: Member Giveback benefits are not guaranteed and have limitations on eligible products
Specific loan terms, APRs, and eligibility requirements not detailed on homepage
Limited information about membership requirements or application process on main page
Does not clearly specify NCUA insurance coverage or specific account protection limits
Geographic headquarters in Great Lakes Bay Region may limit local branch access for remote members
best_for: Members seeking competitive deposit rates and CD yields with low fees
Borrowers looking for quick auto, RV, and personal loan approvals with potential rate discounts
Long-term savers interested in member-owned financial institutions offering year-end rebates
Consumers prioritizing low-fee banking with $0 NSF fees and community-focused lending
diagnosis: Dow Credit Union is best for consumers seeking a full-service credit union with competitive deposit rates, low fees ($0 NSF), and potential year-end member rebates. The main caveat is that membership eligibility, specific loan terms, and which products qualify for Member Giveback programs require direct inquiry, as these details are not fully specified on their website.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Dow — Credit Union in Midland, MI | CreditDoc
meta_description: Dow — NCUA-insured credit union in Midland, MI. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
